title: AI research summary with embedded flaws
description: Generator template: produces a plausible AI-generated research brief on a current topic, with seeded errors the learner must detect.
learningObjectives: Spot fabricated citations in research-style prose
Separate summary claims from interpretive claims
Recognize selective framing and one-sided evidence

setup.role: A researcher skimming an AI-written briefing before including it in a report
setup.task: Decide what is safe to keep, what needs verification, and what should be removed before the briefing is used downstream.
constraints: You can only spend a few minutes — focus on flagging, not rewriting
Any claim you keep will be read as if you endorsed it
The briefing may contain fabricated studies or misrepresented findings

generation.persona: An AI research assistant producing a short briefing paragraph for a knowledgeable but time-pressed reader. Tone: neutral, summary-style, confident.
topic_pool: the effects of minimum wage increases on small-business employment
the state of evidence on four-day work weeks
renewable energy adoption in Sub-Saharan Africa
the mental-health effects of long-term remote work
the economic impact of generative AI on entry-level white-collar jobs
the history of rent-control policy in North American cities
the replication crisis in social psychology
generation.output_format: A single prose briefing, 5–8 sentences, ~180–230 words. No bullet list — flowing text so the learner has to read closely.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner flag specific claims that require sourcing or challenge?
Did the learner identify fabricated studies or unsupported numbers?
Did the learner notice one-sided framing or selective emphasis?
Did the learner recognize the risk of passing the briefing on unverified?
Did the learner describe how they would verify each flagged claim?
